meta.tagline: Packet Tracer conversion intelligence, evidence-grade topology, live ingestion, tenants, jobs, and API tokens

rules.title: Guest VLAN must not access internal networks
Each SSID must map to its expected VLAN
AP/uplink trunks must carry all WLAN VLANs
DHCP scope must match expected VLAN subnet
ACLs must be applied to the correct interface and direction
Client events must map to the expected SSID, VLAN, DHCP scope, and AP trunk

events.detail: Imported Project.pkt and extracted 15 object/evidence entries.
Student roamed from AP-01 to AP-02
Guest isolation expected: internal services denied

evidence.source_text: Synthetic context because no hostname was found.

policy_controls.detail: 0 ACL rule(s) have confirmed interface bindings.
0 ACL line(s) include runtime match counters.
0 VLAN(s) appear configured but not present in trunk forwarding evidence.

metadata.format_note: Native Packet Tracer file. Accuracy is highest when PTEXPLORER_PATH is configured or when configs/show outputs are exported as TXT/ZIP.

command_checklist.command: Export Packet Tracer device configs as TXT or ZIP
command_checklist.why: Native .pkt/.pka binary recovery can miss structured fields; exported configs raise confidence dramatically.
Core configuration, interfaces, VLANs, ACLs, DHCP and routing.
Confirms VLAN IDs, names, status, and switchport hints.
Confirms real trunk membership and allowed VLAN list.
Confirms L3 interfaces, IP addresses, and up/down state.
Confirms segmentation rules and deny/permit enforcement.
Confirms physical adjacency and topology edges.
Confirms blocked/forwarding ports and L2 loop protection.
Confirms secure MAC limits, violations, and sticky MAC behavior.
Confirms bundle health for uplinks and redundant paths.
Confirms non-CDP physical adjacency and multi-vendor topology edges.
Confirms access/trunk port operational status, VLAN placement, duplex, and speed.
Confirms L3 forwarding, default routes, and protocol-learned paths.
Confirms platform/model/IOS evidence for device identity.
Confirms management-plane hardening and weak-service exposure.
Confirms physical-layer stability, CRC/input/output errors, drops and link health.
Confirms whether segmentation rules are receiving runtime traffic matches.
Confirms root bridge, root ports, and L2 forwarding design.
Confirms active L3/NAT/DHCP/HSRP/routing protocol evidence.

quality_gates.name: Operational state coverage
quality_gates.detail: 0 interface status and 0 trunk operation row(s).

quality_gates.name: Runtime validation evidence
quality_gates.detail: 0 ACL counter and 0 interface counter row(s).

quality_gates.name: Line-level traceability
quality_gates.detail: 29% of list objects have source-line evidence.

packet_tracer_profile.analyst_next_step: Export configs/show outputs from Packet Tracer and upload them as TXT or ZIP to move from binary recovery to evidence-grade extraction.

pipeline.detail: Accepted Project.pkt and stored immutable source hash.
Export configs/show outputs from Packet Tracer and upload them as TXT or ZIP to move from binary recovery to evidence-grade extraction.
Parsed devices, interfaces, VLANs, DHCP, ACL, routing, NAT, L2 health, and link hints.
Mapped extracted objects to line-level evidence where possible.

missing_evidence.why: No interface blocks were extracted; topology and ACL binding may be incomplete.
Trunk coverage cannot be validated without trunk evidence.
Segmentation checks cannot confirm deny/permit enforcement without ACL evidence.
DHCP/subnet matching will remain review-only without DHCP pool evidence.
Physical adjacency/topology edges are inferred rather than confirmed.
Interface operational status and L3 reachability cannot be fully verified.
Layer-2 loop/blocked-port behavior cannot be validated.

metadata.format_note: Native Packet Tracer file. Accuracy is highest when PTEXPLORER_PATH is configured or when configs/show outputs are exported as TXT/ZIP.

command_checklist.command: Export Packet Tracer device configs as TXT or ZIP
command_checklist.why: Native .pkt/.pka binary recovery can miss structured fields; exported configs raise confidence dramatically.
Core configuration, interfaces, VLANs, ACLs, DHCP and routing.
Confirms VLAN IDs, names, status, and switchport hints.
Confirms real trunk membership and allowed VLAN list.
Confirms L3 interfaces, IP addresses, and up/down state.
Confirms segmentation rules and deny/permit enforcement.
Confirms physical adjacency and topology edges.
Confirms blocked/forwarding ports and L2 loop protection.
Confirms secure MAC limits, violations, and sticky MAC behavior.
Confirms bundle health for uplinks and redundant paths.
Confirms non-CDP physical adjacency and multi-vendor topology edges.
Confirms access/trunk port operational status, VLAN placement, duplex, and speed.
Confirms L3 forwarding, default routes, and protocol-learned paths.
Confirms platform/model/IOS evidence for device identity.
Confirms management-plane hardening and weak-service exposure.
Confirms physical-layer stability, CRC/input/output errors, drops and link health.
Confirms whether segmentation rules are receiving runtime traffic matches.
Confirms root bridge, root ports, and L2 forwarding design.
Confirms active L3/NAT/DHCP/HSRP/routing protocol evidence.

quality_gates.name: Operational state coverage
quality_gates.detail: 0 interface status and 0 trunk operation row(s).

quality_gates.name: Runtime validation evidence
quality_gates.detail: 0 ACL counter and 0 interface counter row(s).

quality_gates.name: Line-level traceability
quality_gates.detail: 29% of list objects have source-line evidence.

conversion_profile.analyst_next_step: Export configs/show outputs from Packet Tracer and upload them as TXT or ZIP to move from binary recovery to evidence-grade extraction.

rules.name: Guest must be isolated from internal services
rules.condition_expression: ssid.internal_access equals false AND applied ACL deny exists
rules.evidence_required: VLAN gateway + applied ACL + show access-lists
rules.false_positive_guard: Do not pass on ACL text alone; require ip access-group binding on the guest gateway.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Guest VLAN has an inbound/outbound deny to internal subnets and Internet/DNS still allowed.
rules.remediation: Apply guest isolation ACL inbound on the guest VLAN gateway and verify with show access-lists + show running-config interface.

rules.name: Client role must match SSID role
rules.false_positive_guard: Ignore stale client rows older than the active import unless a live event confirms association.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Client role, SSID role, VLAN and DHCP subnet align.
rules.remediation: Fix identity group mapping or move the user to the correct SSID.

rules.name: AP uplink must support expected VLAN
rules.false_positive_guard: Prefer operational trunk evidence over planned AP inventory only.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Every AP serving the SSID has the expected VLAN on its uplink trunk.
rules.remediation: Add missing WLAN VLANs to AP uplink trunks and regenerate validation.

rules.name: Management plane must avoid weak services
rules.condition_expression: no telnet, enable password, insecure HTTP, or exposed SNMP community indicators
rules.false_positive_guard: Flag only when direct config line evidence exists.
rules.acceptance_criteria: SSH-only VTY, enable secret, AAA/local auth, and no insecure HTTP/Telnet evidence.
rules.remediation: Disable Telnet/HTTP, replace enable password with enable secret, restrict SNMP and enforce SSH.

rules.name: Segmentation ACLs should have runtime counters
rules.condition_expression: show access-lists counter evidence exists for enforced segmentation ACLs
rules.evidence_required: show access-lists output with match counters
rules.false_positive_guard: Treat zero counters as Review unless traffic simulation was executed.
rules.acceptance_criteria: At least one active deny/permit ACL line has runtime counter evidence or a documented test result.
rules.remediation: Run a controlled access test, capture show access-lists counters, and attach the evidence.

rules.name: Configured VLANs must be present on operational trunks
rules.condition_expression: configured/access/DHCP VLANs are included in trunk allowed or forwarding evidence
rules.evidence_required: show vlan brief + show interfaces trunk + running-config
rules.false_positive_guard: Do not fail if operational trunk output was not imported; mark Needs Evidence instead.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Every production policy VLAN appears in trunk allowed/forwarding evidence.
rules.remediation: Add missing VLANs to the trunk or update policy inventory, then re-import operational trunk output.

rules.name: Critical uplinks should not show physical error counters
rules.condition_expression: input/output/CRC errors are zero on uplinks and AP trunks
rules.evidence_required: show interfaces counters or show interfaces detail
rules.false_positive_guard: Only escalate when direct counter evidence maps to an uplink/trunk or repeated live events corroborate it.
rules.acceptance_criteria: No CRC/input/output error evidence on critical wireless path interfaces.
rules.remediation: Inspect cable/SFP/duplex, clear counters, monitor again, and document the before/after state.

rules.name: Reports must disclose missing evidence before claiming pass
rules.condition_expression: line-level evidence confidence >= 70% OR missing commands are explicitly listed
rules.evidence_required: conversion readiness score + missing command checklist + evidence profile
rules.false_positive_guard: Do not punish native .pkt uploads; disclose reduced confidence and ask for exported show commands.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Technical report includes readiness score, missing commands, and traceability stats.
rules.remediation: Import the missing show-command bundle or keep the finding in Needs More Evidence.
